prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: What My Sister Buried
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Divorce Gothic
Tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
Theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.
Omen: phone static saying her name
Relationship dynamic: suspiciously healthy handyman friendship
Props: snow globe, unopened letter, scarf with impossible timing

Cast:
- Cole Lark | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=magazine editor | threat_aura=0.29 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=once left town without explaining why
- Derek Bennett | archetype=soft_spoken_menace | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=private school administrator | threat_aura=0.86 | space=church basement described as retreat supplies | secrets=knows exactly how long the sheriff takes to respond; keeps duplicate keys for reasons framed as kindness
- Rachel Pike | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=real estate agent | threat_aura=0.64 | space=meditation annex nobody uses in winter | secrets=once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
- Claire Lark | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=realtor | threat_aura=0.9 | space=dock office with a desk nobody fully claims | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed; inherited documents she should have burned
- Elias Winterson | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=Christmas tree farmer | threat_aura=0.62 | space=toolshed described as seasonal materials | secrets=knows more about the vineyard fire than he admits
- Evelyn Vale | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=local podcaster | threat_aura=0.32 | space=unused portable classroom holding archived disciplinary files | secrets=accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
- Tess Vale | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Brooke Pike | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Derek Bennett is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Cole Lark says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Derek?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Tess. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Cole. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Claire."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."

- expanded_synopsis: string, 180-320 words
